problem_frame: WhatIfInvest needs a credible, distinctive App Store-facing brand asset and the operational scaffolding needed to move from a local Xcode app to first-review readiness. The work is mostly integration and release hygiene around an existing SwiftUI app.

selected_approach.title: Generated fintech icon with Hi_Tomorrow-style ASC scaffold
selected_approach.summary: Generate one polished blue-and-white icon, derive all app icon sizes from it, use the same icon on launch, then port ASC metadata and screenshot/upload/readiness scripts with WhatIfInvest names and first-review env handling.
pros: Fastest path to a coherent first-review package
Keeps brand and launch visuals consistent
Reuses a known local automation pattern
Avoids secrets in git while still giving a concrete env path
cons: Generated icon may need later human brand review
ASC id discovery may be blocked if the app record does not exist in App Store Connect
Screenshot automation may need future UI-test capture work if no raw screenshot exporter exists yet

alternatives.title: Generated fintech icon with Hi_Tomorrow-style ASC scaffold
alternatives.summary: Create the visual asset now and port the release automation scaffold in one pass.
cons: More moving parts than icon-only work

alternatives.title: Code-native simple symbol icon
alternatives.summary: Build a deterministic icon from vector/SVG or drawing code instead of generated bitmap art.
cons: Likely less polished for App Store presentation
Contradicts the request to create a fitting app icon

alternatives.title: Delay automation until ASC record exists
alternatives.summary: Archive first, then add scripts only after the App Store Connect app/version ids are known.
cons: Does not satisfy the request to apply Hi_Tomorrow automation now
Leaves no repeatable review-prep path in the repo

tradeoffs: A generated bitmap icon maximizes polish speed but should be treated as a brand candidate until reviewed in context.
Porting the full Hi_Tomorrow automation shape now creates useful scaffolding even if first-review App Store Connect ids are not yet discoverable.
Screenshot upload scripts can be ported, but WhatIfInvest may need a future screenshot capture/export test before the full ASC preparation path is fully runnable.
Archive verification is valuable even if App Store Connect readiness remains blocked by missing app records or credentials.
key_decisions: Use a blue/white fintech icon with a hindsight-to-growth motif rather than stock-specific logos.
Derive all AppIcon PNGs from a single 1024x1024 source.
Use a launch-screen image asset based on the same icon rather than the current colored square.
Add WhatIfInvest-specific ASC scripts and metadata under the app repo, adapted from Hi_Tomorrow.
Keep `.asc/env.example` committed and `.asc/env` local-only or non-secret with discovered IDs only.
Do not submit the app for review automatically.
assumptions: The Xcode synchronized filesystem group will include new assets and scripts without manual resource phase edits.
The current development team and bundle identifier are intended for first-review setup.
The installed asc CLI and Xcode can be used locally for discovery and archive attempts.
First-review App Store Connect app/version ids may be absent until the app record and version are created.

risks.description: The generated icon may look good at 1024px but lose clarity at small Home Screen sizes.
risks.mitigation: Use a simple high-contrast motif and inspect derived sizes.

risks.description: Archive may fail because signing, provisioning, or app registration is incomplete.
risks.mitigation: Run xcodebuild archive and report the exact blocker; keep scripts ready for retry.

risks.description: ASC discovery cannot fill ids if no App Store Connect app or version exists yet.
risks.mitigation: Create env template and discovery scripts that list apps/versions once the record exists.

risks.description: Hi_Tomorrow has a screenshot capture test and generator; WhatIfInvest may not yet have equivalent raw screenshots.
risks.mitigation: Port upload/readiness scaffolding and document screenshot directory expectations as the minimum first pass.

validation_plan: Generate and inspect the icon source and derived app icon files.
Build the app for simulator after asset and storyboard changes.
Run unit tests if build is healthy.
Run xcodebuild archive for Release on generic iOS destination.
Run ASC discovery/listing scripts and fill `.asc/env` only with discoverable non-secret ids.
Report any App Store Connect setup or screenshot-pipeline blockers explicitly.
